checks.a_analysis: The output contains only information found within the source PDF. All headers, form labels, and rule citations match the source document. There are no fabricated introductions, conclusions, or sections.

All contact information, including URLs, email addresses, phone numbers, and physical addresses, has been verified against the source PDF. Specifically, the agency website URL (https://deq.nc.gov/mfc-proposed-rules), the WebEx meeting link, the event numbers and passwords, and the contact emails for Jennifer Everett and Catherine Blum all match exactly.
The output successfully captures both pages of the source document, including all 14 numbered blocks of the form.
The tables in the output correspond to the structured layout of the form. Table 1 captures blocks 1-6, and Table 2 captures blocks 7-14. No data rows are missing.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: The output fails to capture the selection state of the checkboxes throughout the document. For a completed government form, the specific choices made (represented by 'X' marks in the checkboxes) constitute primary information. The output lists all possible labels but does not indicate which were selected. This occurs in the initial check box section, Block 3 (Proposed Action), Block 5 (Public Hearing planned), and Block 11 (Fiscal Impact).
checks.b_evidence_source: Source document shows specific items checked: 'Notice with a scheduled hearing' (header); 'ADOPTION', 'AMENDMENT', 'READOPTION with substantive changes', and 'REPEAL through READOPTION' (Block 3); 'Yes' (Block 5); 'State funds affected', 'Local funds affected', and 'Approved by OSBM' (Block 11).
checks.b_evidence_output: The output lists the labels (e.g., 'Yes No' in Block 5 or the full list of actions in Block 3) but omits any indication of which checkboxes are marked, resulting in a loss of the document's primary data.
checks.b_evidence_location: Page 1 and Page 2, throughout various sections/tables.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The text content, including the extensive lists of rule citations in blocks 3, 8, and 11, was found to be highly accurate with no meaningful wording changes or omissions beyond the systemic checkbox issue already flagged.

checks.a_analysis: I reviewed the source PDF and the output JSON. The only repeated text is 'Notice of Text 0300 – 04/2020', which appears in the source as a footer on page 1, a header on page 2, and a footer on page 2. The output correctly reflects this repetition as separate elements at the appropriate points in the document flow. No other content duplication (e.g., paragraphs or list items appearing twice) was found.

The output uses a single Heading 2 for the document title 'NOTICE OF TEXT [Authority G.S. 150B-21.2(c)]'. There are no other headings; the rest of the form's structure is handled via tables representing the numbered blocks. This is a logical structural choice for a form. There are no skipped heading levels (e.g., H1 to H3).

The form's numbered blocks (1-6 on page 1 and 7-14 on page 2) are rendered as two distinct tables. The structural integrity of these tables is maintained, with rows and columns correctly identifying the distinct numbered sections. For example, on page 2, blocks 12 and 13 are correctly placed side-by-side in a single row with two cells, mirroring the source layout.

The reading order in the JSON follows the logical sequence of the source PDF, starting from the title and 'OAH USE ONLY' box, proceeding through the checkboxes, and then sequentially through the numbered blocks (1 through 14). Reordered header/footer elements for page transition are placed logically.
The checkboxes at the top of page 1 are correctly converted into an unordered list in the output (index 6). The nesting and items accurately reflect the source content.

The output contains all links present in the source document, including agency website URLs, the WebEx meeting link, and contact email addresses. The URLs are correct and properly identified as links with appropriate 'href' attributes.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output maintains the logical structure and sequence of the source PDF. The boxed form layout from the PDF is converted into a linear web-form style with clearly defined sections (Blocks 1-14). The two-column layout for blocks 12 and 13 is preserved in the HTML. The visual organization allows a user to find information in the same relative positions as the source.

The document is a form rather than a set of data tables. The form structure, including the specific two-column arrangement for blocks 12 and 13 at the end of the document, is correctly rendered in the HTML. Lists of rules within the form are readable and correctly associated with their respective sections.
The main meaningful image, the State of North Carolina Office of Administrative Hearings seal, is present and correctly rendered at the top of the HTML document.

There is no evidence of body content duplication. Section headings like "Notice of Text 0300 – 04/2020" appear multiple times, but this accurately reflects their occurrence in the source PDF (as headers/footers on both pages). The actual form content (blocks 1-14) is presented once in sequence.
